prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetheart, a CPU is what people call the “brain” of the computer. It stands for Central Processing Unit, but you don’t have to remember all that unless you want to impress someone! Basically, it’s the part that does all the thinking and tells the other parts what to do. When you open a game or type a letter, the CPU is working hard behind the scenes to make sure everything happens in the right order. I may not know exactly how all the little pieces inside work, but I do know that without the CPU, the computer wouldn’t know what to do with itself!
